methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mimic Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company, a spinout from ETH Zurich, that has raised $16M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a broader Robotics & Manufacturing peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Given the early stage and the absence of sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting narrow filtering criteria, the Peer-Median approach is not applicable. Instead, the analysis relies on Stage-Progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $16M raised baseline. This yields an implied valuation of $40M.

The triangulated range anchors to $40M as the median point, with a low bound of $34M and a high bound of $46M. This single-method conclusion introduces material uncertainty. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 2.5x multiplier to Mimic's specific market position and technology maturity relative to earlier-stage robotics companies, as well as broader sector sentiment in hardware-intensive automation. The valuation would benefit from refinement as the company approaches Series A and develops a more densely populated peer comparison set.
